sentences.sentence: In 1877, Giovanni Schiaparelli, an Italian astronomer, made drawings and maps of the Martian surface that suggested strange features.
sentences.translation: 1877 年，意大利天文学家乔瓦尼·斯基亚帕雷利绘制了火星表面的图画和地图，其中呈现出一些奇特的特征。

structure.text: Giovanni Schiaparelli, an Italian astronomer
made drawings and maps of the Martian surface that suggested strange features

difficult_points: an Italian astronomer 是 Giovanni Schiaparelli 的同位语。
that suggested strange features 是定语从句，修饰 drawings and maps。

sentences.sentence: The images from telescopes at this time were not as sharp as today's.
sentences.translation: 当时通过望远镜看到的图像不如今天的清晰。

structure.text: The images from telescopes at this time
were not as sharp as today's

difficult_points: not as ... as 表示前者在程度上不及后者。
today's 是名词性所有格，省略了 images。

sentences.sentence: Schiaparelli said he could see a network of lines, or canali.
sentences.translation: 斯基亚帕雷利说，他能看到一个线条网络，也就是所谓的 canali。

structure.text: Schiaparelli said
he could see a network of lines, or canali

difficult_points: said 后省略了引导宾语从句的 that。
or canali 是同位解释；canali 是意大利语，意为沟道或水道。

sentences.sentence: In 1894, an American astronomer, Percival Lowell, made a series of observations of Mars from his own observations of Mars from his own observatory at Flagstaff, Arizona, USA.
sentences.translation: 1894 年，美国天文学家珀西瓦尔·洛厄尔在自己位于美国亚利桑那州弗拉格斯塔夫的天文台对火星进行了一系列观测。

structure.text: an American astronomer, Percival Lowell
made a series of observations of Mars from his own observations of Mars from his own observatory at Flagstaff, Arizona, USA

difficult_points: Percival Lowell 是 an American astronomer 的同位语。
from his own observations of Mars 在原文中疑似误重复；按语义应重点理解为他从自己的天文台观测火星。

sentences.sentence: Lowell was convinced a great network of canals had been dug to irrigate crops for the Martian race!
sentences.translation: 洛厄尔深信，火星人为了灌溉农作物而开凿了庞大的运河网络！

structure.text: Lowell was convinced
a great network of canals had been dug
to irrigate crops for the Martian race

difficult_points: was convinced 后省略 that，后接他深信的内容。
had been dug 是过去完成时的被动语态，to irrigate 表示开凿目的。

sentences.sentence: He suggested that each canal had fertile vegetation on either side, making them noticeable from Earth.
sentences.translation: 他认为，每条运河两侧都有茂盛的植被，因此人们从地球上也能注意到它们。

structure.text: He suggested
that each canal had fertile vegetation on either side
making them noticeable from Earth

difficult_points: making 引导现在分词结果状语，说明植被带来的可见效果。
them 语法上可指 canals，结合语境指带植被的运河线条。

sentences.sentence: Drawings and globes he made show a network of canals and oases all over the planet.
sentences.translation: 他制作的图画和球形模型显示，运河与绿洲组成的网络遍布火星。

structure.text: Drawings and globes he made
show
a network of canals and oases all over the planet

difficult_points: he made 是省略关系代词 that 的定语从句，修饰 Drawings and globes。
all over the planet 修饰网络的分布范围。

sentences.sentence: The idea that there was intelligent life on Mars gained strength in the late 19th century.
sentences.translation: 19 世纪末，火星上存在智慧生命的观点越来越有影响力。

structure.text: The idea that there was intelligent life on Mars
gained strength
in the late 19th century

difficult_points: that 从句是 idea 的同位语从句，具体说明观点内容。
gained strength 表示观点获得更多支持，而非体力增强。

sentences.sentence: In 1898, H.G. Wells wrote a science fiction classic, The War of the Worlds about an invading force of Martians who try to conquer Earth.
sentences.translation: 1898 年，H.G. 威尔斯创作了科幻经典《世界大战》，讲述一支试图征服地球的火星入侵军。

structure.text: H.G. Wells
wrote a science fiction classic, The War of the Worlds about an invading force of Martians who try to conquer Earth

difficult_points: The War of the Worlds 是 a science fiction classic 的同位语。
who try to conquer Earth 是定语从句，修饰 Martians。

sentences.sentence: They use highly advanced technology (advanced for 1898) to crush human resistance in their path.
sentences.translation: 他们使用高度先进的技术——至少以 1898 年的标准来看如此——来粉碎沿途人类的抵抗。

structure.text: They
use highly advanced technology (advanced for 1898)
to crush human resistance in their path

difficult_points: They 指小说中的火星入侵者。
括号中的 advanced for 1898 限定“先进”是相对于当时而言。

sentences.sentence: In 1917, Edgar Rice Burroughs wrote the first in a series of 11 novels about Mars.
sentences.translation: 1917 年，埃德加·赖斯·巴勒斯写出了火星题材十一部系列小说中的第一部。

structure.text: Edgar Rice Burroughs
wrote the first in a series of 11 novels about Mars

difficult_points: the first 后省略 novel，靠 in a series of 11 novels 补足含义。
about Mars 后置修饰 novels。

expressions.text: the first in a series of

sentences.sentence: Strange beings and rampaging Martian monsters gripped the public's imagination.
sentences.translation: 奇异生物和横冲直撞的火星怪兽牢牢吸引了公众的想象力。

structure.text: Strange beings and rampaging Martian monsters
gripped
the public's imagination

difficult_points: rampaging 是现在分词作定语，表示怪兽狂暴肆虐。
gripped the public's imagination 是比喻，表示强烈吸引公众。

expressions.meaning_zh: 牢牢吸引公众的想象力

sentences.sentence: A radio broadcast by Orson Welles on Halloween night in 1938 of The War of the Worlds caused widespread panic across America.
sentences.translation: 1938 年万圣节之夜，奥森·威尔斯播出的《世界大战》广播剧在全美引发了广泛恐慌。

structure.text: A radio broadcast by Orson Welles on Halloween night in 1938 of The War of the Worlds
caused widespread panic
across America

difficult_points: 多个介词短语共同后置修饰 A radio broadcast，分别说明播出者、时间和内容。
caused 的宾语是 widespread panic。

sentences.sentence: People ran into the streets in their pyjamas-millions believed the dramatic reports of a Martian invasion.
sentences.translation: 人们穿着睡衣跑上街头——数百万人相信了那些关于火星入侵的戏剧化报道。

structure.text: People ran into the streets in their pyjamas
millions believed the dramatic reports of a Martian invasion

difficult_points: pyjamas-millions 中的连字符实际承担破折号作用，连接两个独立分句。
millions 在此独立作主语，意为数百万人。

sentences.sentence: Probes are very important to our understanding of other planets.
sentences.translation: 探测器对于我们理解其他行星至关重要。

structure.text: Probes
are very important
to our understanding of other planets

difficult_points: to our understanding 表示“对于我们的理解而言”。
understanding of other planets 中 of 引出理解的对象。

expressions.text: be important to our understanding of

sentences.sentence: Much of our recent knowledge comes from these robotic missions into space.
sentences.translation: 我们近来获得的许多知识都来自这些机器人太空任务。

structure.text: Much of our recent knowledge
comes from
these robotic missions into space

difficult_points: Much of 后接不可数名词 knowledge。
robotic missions 指由无人机器人探测器执行的任务。

sentences.sentence: The first images sent back from Mars came from Mariner 4 in July 1965.
sentences.translation: 最早从火星传回的图像来自 1965 年 7 月的“水手 4 号”。

structure.text: The first images sent back from Mars
came from Mariner 4
in July 1965

difficult_points: sent back from Mars 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 images。
came from 表示图像由 Mariner 4 获取并传回。

sentences.sentence: They showed a cratered and barren landscape, more like the surface of our moon than Earth.
sentences.translation: 这些图像显示出一片遍布陨石坑、荒芜不毛的地貌，与其说像地球，不如说更像月球表面。

structure.text: They
showed a cratered and barren landscape
more like the surface of our moon than Earth

difficult_points: They 指上一句的 images。
more like A than B 表示“与 B 相比更像 A”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 遍布陨石坑的荒芜地貌

sentences.sentence: In 1969, Mariners 6 and 7 were launched and took 200 photographs of Mars's southern hemisphere and pole on fly-by missions.
sentences.translation: 1969 年，“水手 6 号”和“水手 7 号”发射升空，并在飞越任务中拍摄了 200 张火星南半球及南极地区的照片。

structure.text: Mariners 6 and 7
were launched and took 200 photographs of Mars's southern hemisphere and pole on fly-by missions

difficult_points: were launched 是被动语态，took 则是主动谓语，两者由 and 并列。
on fly-by missions 说明拍照发生在飞越而非着陆任务中。

sentences.sentence: But these showed little more information.
sentences.translation: 但这些照片没有提供多少新的信息。

structure.text: But
these
showed little more information

difficult_points: these 指上一句的 200 photographs。
little more information 表示新增信息很少，little 带否定意味。

sentences.sentence: In 1971, Mariner 9's mission was to orbit the planet every 12 hours.
sentences.translation: 1971 年，“水手 9 号”的任务是每 12 小时绕火星运行一周。

structure.text: Mariner 9's mission
was to orbit the planet every 12 hours

difficult_points: to orbit ... 是不定式作表语，说明 mission 的具体内容。
the planet 在本段语境中指 Mars。

sentences.sentence: In 1975, The USA sent two Viking probes to the planet, each with a lander and an orbiter.
sentences.translation: 1975 年，美国向火星发射了两艘“海盗号”探测器，每艘都配有一个着陆器和一个轨道器。

structure.text: The USA sent two Viking probes to the planet
each with a lander and an orbiter

difficult_points: each 指 two Viking probes 中的每一艘。
with 短语补充说明每艘探测器的组成。

sentences.sentence: The Landers had sampler arms to scoop up Maritain rocks and did experiments to try and find signs of life.
sentences.translation: 着陆器配有取样臂，用来铲取火星岩石，并开展实验以寻找生命迹象。

structure.text: The Landers
had sampler arms to scoop up Maritain rocks
and did experiments to try and find signs of life

difficult_points: 两个 to 不定式分别说明取样臂用途和进行实验的目的。
Maritain 是原文拼写错误，应按 Martian（火星的）理解。

sentences.sentence: Although no life was found, they sent back the first colour pictures of the planet's surface and atmosphere from pivoting cameras.
sentences.translation: 虽然没有发现生命，但它们通过可转动的相机传回了首批火星表面和大气的彩色照片。

structure.text: Although no life was found
they sent back the first colour pictures of the planet's surface and atmosphere
from pivoting cameras

difficult_points: Although 从句与主句构成让步关系。
they 指两艘 Viking probes 或其着陆器；pivoting 表示相机可以转动。

sentences.sentence: The Martian meteorite found in Earth aroused doubts to the above analysis.
sentences.translation: 在地球上发现的火星陨石使人们对上述分析产生了怀疑。

structure.text: The Martian meteorite found in Earth
aroused doubts
to the above analysis

difficult_points: found in Earth 是过去分词短语，修饰 meteorite；更自然的搭配通常是 found on Earth。
aroused doubts to 搭配不自然，语义应为“引发对上述分析的质疑”。

sentences.sentence: ALH84001 meteorite was discovered in December 1984 in Antarctica, by members of the ANSMET project; The sample was ejected from Mars about 17 million years ago and spent 11,000 years in or on the Antarctic ice sheets.
sentences.translation: 1984 年 12 月，ANSMET 项目成员在南极洲发现了 ALH84001 陨石；这块样本约在 1700 万年前从火星喷出，随后在南极冰盖内或表面停留了 1.1 万年。

structure.text: ALH84001 meteorite was discovered in December 1984 in Antarctica, by members of the ANSMET project
The sample was ejected from Mars about 17 million years ago
and spent 11,000 years in or on the Antarctic ice sheets

difficult_points: 分号连接两个独立主句；was discovered 与 was ejected 均为被动语态。
spent 的主语仍是 The sample；in or on 保留了样本位于冰层内部或表面的两种可能。

sentences.sentence: Composition analysis by NASA revealed a kind of magnetite that on Earth, is only found in association with certain microorganisms.
sentences.translation: 美国国家航空航天局的成分分析发现了一种磁铁矿；在地球上，这种矿物只会与某些微生物共同出现。

structure.text: Composition analysis by NASA
revealed a kind of magnetite
that on Earth, is only found in association with certain microorganisms

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 magnetite；on Earth 是从句内前置的地点状语。
be found in association with 表示与某物伴生或相关出现。

sentences.sentence: Some structures resembling the mineralized casts of terrestrial bacteria and their appendages fibrils or by-products occur in the rims of carbonate globules and pre-terrestrial aqueous alteration regions.
sentences.translation: 在碳酸盐小球的边缘及其抵达地球前发生水蚀变化的区域中，出现了一些结构，类似地球细菌及其附属物的矿化铸型、纤维或副产物。

structure.text: Some structures resembling the mineralized casts of terrestrial bacteria and their appendages fibrils or by-products
occur
in the rims of carbonate globules and pre-terrestrial aqueous alteration regions

difficult_points: resembling 是现在分词，后置修饰 Some structures。
their appendages fibrils or by-products 缺少必要标点，列举关系含混；翻译按可辨的并列项目处理。

sentences.sentence: The size and shape of the objects is consistent with Earthly fossilized nanobacteria but the existence of nanobacteria itself is still controversial.
sentences.translation: 这些物体的大小和形状与地球上的纳米细菌化石相符，但纳米细菌本身是否存在仍有争议。

structure.text: The size and shape of the objects is consistent with Earthly fossilized nanobacteria
but the existence of nanobacteria itself is still controversial

difficult_points: but 连接“形态相符”与“对象本身仍有争议”两个相反信息。
The size and shape 是并列主语，原文却使用单数 is，存在主谓一致问题。

sentences.sentence: In 1965, the Mariner 4 probe discovered that Mars had no global magnetic field that would protect the planet from potentially life-threatening cosmic radiation and solar radiation; observations made in the late 1990s by the Mars Global Surveyor confirmed this discovery.
sentences.translation: 1965 年，“水手 4 号”探测器发现火星不存在能够保护整个星球免受潜在致命宇宙辐射和太阳辐射的全球磁场；20 世纪 90 年代末“火星全球勘测者号”的观测证实了这一发现。

structure.text: In 1965, the Mariner 4 probe discovered that Mars had no global magnetic field that would protect the planet from potentially life-threatening cosmic radiation and solar radiation
observations made in the late 1990s by the Mars Global Surveyor
confirmed this discovery

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导 discovered 的宾语从句，第二个 that 引导定语从句修饰 magnetic field。
made in the late 1990s ... 是过去分词短语，修饰 observations。

sentences.sentence: Scientists speculate that the lack of magnetic shielding helped the solar wind blow away much of Mars's atmosphere over the course of several billion years.
sentences.translation: 科学家推测，缺少磁场屏障使太阳风在数十亿年间吹走了火星大部分大气。

structure.text: Scientists speculate
that the lack of magnetic shielding helped the solar wind blow away much of Mars's atmosphere
over the course of several billion years

difficult_points: helped the solar wind blow away 是 help + 宾语 + 动词原形结构。
over the course of 表示漫长过程中的时间跨度。

sentences.sentence: After mapping cosmic radiation levels at various depths on Mars, researchers have concluded that any life within the first several meters of the planet's surface would be killed by lethal doses of cosmic radiation.
sentences.translation: 在绘制火星不同深度的宇宙辐射水平后，研究人员得出结论：火星表面以下最初几米内的任何生命都会被致命剂量的宇宙辐射杀死。

structure.text: After mapping cosmic radiation levels at various depths on Mars
researchers have concluded
that any life within the first several meters of the planet's surface would be killed by lethal doses of cosmic radiation

difficult_points: After mapping 的逻辑主语是 researchers。
would be killed 是情态动词加被动语态，说明推断结果。

sentences.sentence: In 2007, it was calculated that DNA and RNA damage by cosmic radiation would limit life on Mars to depths greater than 7.5 metres below the planet's surface.
sentences.translation: 2007 年，有计算表明，宇宙辐射对 DNA 和 RNA 的破坏会使火星生命只能存在于地表以下超过 7.5 米的深处。

structure.text: it was calculated
that DNA and RNA damage by cosmic radiation would limit life on Mars to depths greater than 7.5 metres below the planet's surface

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，that 从句才是实际计算内容。
limit ... to ... 表示把生命可能存在的范围限制在特定深度。

sentences.sentence: Therefore, the best potential locations for discovering life on Mars may be at subsurface environments that have not been studied yet.
sentences.translation: 因此，最有可能发现火星生命的地点或许是那些尚未研究的地下环境。

structure.text: Therefore
the best potential locations for discovering life on Mars
may be at subsurface environments that have not been studied yet

difficult_points: for discovering life on Mars 后置修饰 locations，说明其用途。
that have not been studied yet 是定语从句，修饰 subsurface environments。

sentences.sentence: Disappearance of the magnetic field may played an significant role in the process of Martian climate change.
sentences.translation: 磁场的消失可能在火星气候变化过程中发挥了重要作用。

structure.text: Disappearance of the magnetic field
may played an significant role
in the process of Martian climate change

difficult_points: may played 存在语法错误，情态动词 may 后通常应使用 play 或 have played。
an significant 的冠词错误，significant 前通常用 a；原文均予以保留。

sentences.sentence: According to the valuation of the scientists, the climate of Mars gradually transits from warm and wet to cold and dry after magnetic field vanished.
sentences.translation: 据科学家评估，磁场消失后，火星气候逐渐从温暖湿润转变为寒冷干燥。

structure.text: According to the valuation of the scientists
the climate of Mars gradually transits from warm and wet to cold and dry
after magnetic field vanished

difficult_points: from warm and wet to cold and dry 构成 from ... to ... 的状态变化。
after magnetic field vanished 缺少冠词 the；transits 用词生硬，按“转变”理解。

sentences.sentence: NASA's recent missions have focused on another question: whether Mars held lakes or oceans of liquid water on its surface in the ancient past.
sentences.translation: NASA 近期的任务聚焦于另一个问题：远古时期的火星表面是否曾有液态水形成的湖泊或海洋。

structure.text: NASA's recent missions
have focused on another question
whether Mars held lakes or oceans of liquid water on its surface in the ancient past

difficult_points: 冒号后的 whether 从句具体说明 another question 的内容。
held 在此表示火星表面“拥有、存在”湖泊或海洋。

sentences.sentence: Scientists have found hematite, a mineral that forms in the presence of water.
sentences.translation: 科学家发现了赤铁矿，这是一种会在有水环境中形成的矿物。

structure.text: Scientists have found hematite
a mineral that forms in the presence of water

difficult_points: a mineral 是 hematite 的同位语。
that forms in the presence of water 是定语从句，修饰 mineral。

sentences.sentence: Thus, the mission of the Mars Exploration Rovers of 2004 was not to look for present or past life, but for evidence of liquid water on the surface of Mars in the planet's ancient past.
sentences.translation: 因此，2004 年火星探测车的任务不是寻找现存或过去的生命，而是寻找远古火星表面存在液态水的证据。

structure.text: Thus
the mission of the Mars Exploration Rovers of 2004
was not to look for present or past life, but for evidence of liquid water on the surface of Mars in the planet's ancient past

difficult_points: not ... but ... 对比任务不做什么与真正寻找什么。
but 后省略了重复的 to look，完整语义是 but to look for evidence。

sentences.sentence: Liquid water, necessary for Earth life and for metabolism as generally conducted by species on Earth, cannot exist on the surface of Mars under its present low atmospheric pressure and temperature, except at the lowest shaded elevations for short periods and liquid water does not appear at the surface itself.
sentences.translation: 液态水是地球生命以及地球物种通常进行的新陈代谢所必需的；在火星目前的低气压和低温条件下，它无法存在于地表，只有最低处的阴影区域可能短暂例外，而且液态水并不会真正出现在地表。

structure.text: Liquid water, necessary for Earth life and for metabolism as generally conducted by species on Earth
cannot exist on the surface of Mars under its present low atmospheric pressure and temperature, except at the lowest shaded elevations for short periods
and liquid water does not appear at the surface itself

difficult_points: necessary ... 是形容词短语，补充说明 Liquid water；as conducted 修饰 metabolism。
except at ... for short periods 限定前述不能存在的例外；and 后又并列一个完整分句。

sentences.sentence: In March 2004, NASA announced that its rover Opportunity had discovered evidence that Mars was, in the ancient past, a wet planet.
sentences.translation: 2004 年 3 月，NASA 宣布，“机遇号”火星车已经发现证据，表明火星在远古时期曾是一颗湿润的行星。

structure.text: In March 2004, NASA announced
that its rover Opportunity had discovered evidence
that Mars was, in the ancient past, a wet planet

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导 announced 的宾语从句，第二个 that 说明 evidence 的具体内容。
had discovered 使用过去完成时，表示发现早于 NASA 的宣布。

sentences.sentence: This had raised hopes that evidence of past life might be found on the planet today.
sentences.translation: 这一发现使人们燃起希望：如今或许能在这颗行星上找到过去存在生命的证据。

structure.text: This
had raised hopes
that evidence of past life might be found on the planet today

difficult_points: This 指上一句关于古代火星湿润环境的证据。
that 从句说明 hopes 的内容；might be found 是情态动词加被动语态。

sentences.sentence:
ESA confirmed that the Mars Express orbiter had directly detected huge reserves of water ice at
Mars' south pole in January 2004.
sentences.translation: 欧洲航天局证实，“火星快车号”轨道器已于 2004 年 1 月在火星南极直接探测到大量水冰储备。

structure.text: ESA confirmed
that the Mars Express orbiter had directly detected huge reserves of water ice

at
Mars' south pole in January 2004

difficult_points: had directly detected 是过去完成时，表示探测发生在“证实”之前。
原文在 at 与 Mars' 之间有换行，这是版面换行而非句法断点。

sentences.sentence: Researchers from the Center of Astrobiology (Spain) and the Catholic University of the North in Chile have found an 'oasis' of microorganisms two meters below the surface of the Atacama Desert, SOLID, a detector for signs of life which could be used in environments similar to subsoil on Mars.
sentences.translation: 西班牙天体生物学中心和智利北方天主教大学的研究人员在阿塔卡马沙漠地表下两米处发现了一个微生物“绿洲”；SOLID 是一种生命迹象探测器，可用于类似火星地下土壤的环境。

structure.text: Researchers from the Center of Astrobiology (Spain) and the Catholic University of the North in Chile
have found an 'oasis' of microorganisms two meters below the surface of the Atacama Desert
SOLID, a detector for signs of life which could be used in environments similar to subsoil on Mars

difficult_points: 逗号后的 SOLID 与前句缺少连接谓语，疑有原文脱漏；可辨含义是它是一种生命探测器。
which could be used ... 是定语从句，修饰 a detector for signs of life。

expressions.text: a detector for signs of life

sentences.sentence: "We have named it a 'microbial oasis' because we found microorganisms developing in a habitat that was rich in rock salt and other highly hygroscopic compounds that absorb water" explained Victor Parro, researcher from the Center of Astrobiology in Spain.
sentences.translation: “我们把它称为‘微生物绿洲’，因为我们发现微生物正在一个富含岩盐及其他强吸湿性化合物的环境中生长，这些化合物能够吸收水分。”西班牙天体生物学中心研究员维克托·帕罗解释道。

structure.text: We have named it a 'microbial oasis'
because we found microorganisms developing in a habitat that was rich in rock salt and other highly hygroscopic compounds that absorb water
explained Victor Parro, researcher from the Center of Astrobiology in Spain

difficult_points: found microorganisms developing 是 find + 宾语 + 现在分词，表示发现微生物正在生长。
两个 that 从句分别修饰 habitat 和 compounds；researcher ... 是 Victor Parro 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: "If there are similar microbes on Mars or remains in similar conditions to the ones we have found in Atacama, we could detect them with instruments like SOLID" Parro highlighted.
sentences.translation: “如果火星上存在类似的微生物，或有处于类似环境中的遗骸，我们就能用 SOLID 这样的仪器探测到它们。”帕罗强调说。

structure.text: If there are similar microbes on Mars or remains in similar conditions to the ones we have found in Atacama
we could detect them with instruments like SOLID
Parro highlighted

difficult_points: If 从句中 similar microbes 与 remains 是 there are 后的并列存在物。
we have found in Atacama 是省略关系代词 that 的定语从句，修饰 the ones；them 指 microbes or remains。

sentences.sentence: Even more intriguing, however, is the alternative scenario by Spanish scientists: If those samples could be found to that use DNA, as Earthly life does, as their genetic code.
sentences.translation: 然而，西班牙科学家提出的另一种设想更加耐人寻味：如果能发现那些像地球生命一样使用 DNA 作为遗传密码的样本。

structure.text: Even more intriguing, however, is the alternative scenario by Spanish scientists
If those samples could be found to that use DNA, as Earthly life does, as their genetic code

difficult_points: 主句为表语前置倒装，正常语序是 the alternative scenario ... is even more intriguing。
If 从句中的 to that 明显为误植，导致语法残缺；按上下文应描述“发现使用 DNA 的样本”，原文仍保留。

sentences.sentence: It is extremely unlikely that such a highly specialised, complex molecule like DNA could have evolved separately on the two planets, indicating that there must be a common origin for Martian and Earthly life.
sentences.translation: 像 DNA 这样高度专门化而复杂的分子极不可能在两颗行星上各自独立演化出来，这意味着火星生命与地球生命必定拥有共同起源。

structure.text: It is extremely unlikely
that such a highly specialised, complex molecule like DNA could have evolved separately on the two planets
indicating that there must be a common origin for Martian and Earthly life

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，that 从句是真正被判断为不可能的内容。
indicating 是结果性现在分词，内部 that 从句作 indicating 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Life based on DNA first appeared on Mars and then spread to Earth, where it then evolved into the myriad forms of plants and creatures that exist today.
sentences.translation: 以 DNA 为基础的生命最初出现在火星，随后传播到地球，并在这里演化成今天存在的无数植物和生物形态。

structure.text: Life based on DNA
first appeared on Mars and then spread to Earth
where it then evolved into the myriad forms of plants and creatures that exist today

difficult_points: based on DNA 是过去分词短语，修饰 Life。
where 指 Earth；从句内部 that exist today 修饰 plants and creatures。

sentences.sentence: If this was found to be the case, we would have to face the logical conclusion: we are all Martian.
sentences.translation: 如果事实果真如此，我们就不得不面对一个合乎逻辑的结论：我们都是火星人。

structure.text: If this was found to be the case
we would have to face the logical conclusion
we are all Martian

difficult_points: If + 过去式与 would 构成假设条件，讨论尚未证实的情形。
冒号后的独立分句具体说明 the logical conclusion。

expressions.text: be found to be the case

sentences.sentence: If not, we would continue to search the life of signs.
sentences.translation: 如果并非如此，我们仍会继续寻找生命迹象。

structure.text: If not
we would continue to search the life of signs

difficult_points: If not 是省略结构，完整含义是“如果情况不是这样”。
the life of signs 词序明显错误，应按 signs of life 理解，但原文不作改动。
